label: Lip, oral cavity
quick_take: Two very different stories sit under the same label. The "classic" Western lip cancer — high in agricultural outdoor populations (Canada, Malta, Italy) — is collapsing, while intra-oral cancer in South Asia and East China is either exploding or simply running at astonishing rates.

findings.title: Singapore Indian women follow a perfect U-shape
findings.text: Vol III → X: 37.5 → 4.7 per 100 000, then rebound to 5.5 in vol XII. Same cohort, same registry, 85 % fall and partial recovery — exactly the kind of pattern the Shanghai cervix plot highlighted. The ASR in vol III (37.5) is 8.8× the global female median — tied for top spatial outlier in the dataset. Ethnic-Indian case counts are small (min 12 in a volume); crude rate is roughly flat, so the fall is genuine decline plus substantial age-structure change in the subgroup.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — Singapore Indian women follow a perfect U-shape

findings.title: Canadian lip cancer disappearing
findings.text: Newfoundland men fell 38.3 → 11.8 (−69 %), Saskatchewan 36.7 → 9.3 (−75 %), New Brunswick 34.7 → 11.3 (−68 %), all through a characteristic U (high-high-drop-bottom-small rebound). Plausibly fewer outdoor fishermen/farmers + UV protection — the lip vermilion component.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — Canadian lip cancer disappearing

findings.title: Inland-China intra-oral emergence
findings.text: Jiashan County females ASR +93 % (n=26→71), Qidong +70 %, Harbin flat-to-down, Wuhan +5 % since vol VIII. Shanghai men's ASR fell 38 % but the crude rose 46 %, i.e. Shanghai's population aged rapidly — some of the cross-city comparison is population structure rather than biology.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — Inland-China intra-oral emergence

findings.title: India & Pakistan dominate absolute levels
findings.text: Kamrup Urban (Assam) males 59.5, Meghalaya 52.3, Pakistan South Karachi women 23.8 — all 5–6× the world median. Betel quid / gutkha belt.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — India & Pakistan dominate absolute levels

findings.title: USA ethnic gaps are enormous
findings.text: In vol IV–V the US male top-to-bottom ratio across ethnicities was 27×; US vol V women had a 21× spread. The highest male subgroup was SF-Bay Black (ethnic 30), the lowest was Hispanic (42).
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — USA ethnic gaps are enormous

findings.title: Bimodal age curves in Algeria and Jiashan
findings.text: the female curve has a visible low-age hump before the usual 60+ peak, which is unusual for oral cancer.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — Bimodal age curves in Algeria and Jiashan

findings.text: None of the main findings ride on coding breaks for this site.
Uganda Kyadondo's +1548 % male change must be read with the vol-VII pop_warning — likely reflects population-denominator change.
US ethnic subgroup spreads before vol VI should be read cautiously (small subgroup pops, early vol).
Document the Newfoundland/Saskatchewan/Malta lip collapse as a coherent Western-lip-vermilion story.
